summary: Implementation plan for the 1985-NY-Yankees Azure Function that nightly queries GPT-4o via TRAPI and persists the structured roster to Azure Blob Storage using Managed Identity. The plan covers IaC provisioning, TRAPI integration, blob writer, schema validation, Timer Trigger configuration, observability, CI/CD with staging slot swap, and documentation.
assumptions: TRAPI supports Azure AD Managed Identity bearer tokens; if not, a Key Vault secret fallback is used.
TRAPI base URL and GPT-4o model version will be confirmed by rciapala before APP-01 begins.
Player scope is limited to the 25-man active roster (24–28 members) unless owner specifies otherwise.
TRAPI is reachable over the public internet; no VNet integration is required for initial deployment.
Consumption Plan is acceptable; strict cold-start SLA is not a requirement for the 2 AM execution.
GitHub Actions OIDC federation is already available or can be enabled for the target Azure subscription.
Existing Bicep modules in /infra (storage, functionapp, rbac, monitoring, alerts) will be extended rather than replaced.
Python 3.11 runtime is confirmed available in the target Azure region on Consumption Plan.
The 'staging' deployment slot is available on the Consumption Plan (requires Standard or higher; if not, a separate staging Function App will be used).
Application Insights workspace-based instance is provisioned by infra and wired via APPLICATIONINSIGHTS_CONNECTION_STRING.
open_questions: Does TRAPI support Azure AD Managed Identity bearer tokens, or is an API key required (Key Vault storage)?
What is the TRAPI base URL and GPT-4o model version/API version to pin?
Should the roster include only the 25-man active roster or also coaching staff, injured list, and front-office personnel?
Is TRAPI network-restricted (requiring VNet integration and private endpoints)?
Is there a strict SLA on the 2 AM execution time that would require Premium Plan to avoid cold-start risk?
Does the Consumption Plan in the target subscription support deployment slots, or is a separate staging Function App needed?

waves.name: Foundation
waves.objective: Resolve blockers, provision all cloud infrastructure, and establish CI/CD pipeline so engineers can deploy and test incrementally.
entry_criteria: spec.md reviewed and open questions triaged
Azure subscription and resource group available
GitHub Actions OIDC / service principal credentials configured
TRAPI endpoint and auth mechanism confirmed (or assumption documented)
exit_criteria: All Bicep infrastructure deployed to staging environment without errors
Managed Identity RBAC assignments verified (Storage Blob Data Contributor, Key Vault Secrets User)
GitHub Actions CI pipeline executes lint and unit tests on PR
CD pipeline deploys to staging slot on merge to main
Task plan approved

waves.name: Core Build
waves.objective: Implement the core function logic: TRAPI client, prompt template, schema validation, blob writer, Timer Trigger, and structured logging.
entry_criteria: TRAPI endpoint URL and auth scope confirmed
Prompt template content agreed (player scope confirmed)
exit_criteria: Function executes end-to-end in staging: TRAPI query → validation → blob write
Failed/invalid responses written to failed/ prefix and alert fires
Unit tests pass for TRAPI client, schema validator, and blob writer
Structured log events emitted for all key checkpoints
Custom metric player_count_returned emitted on successful run

waves.name: Hardening
waves.objective: Add reliability controls, security hardening, observability alerting, and integration tests to production-harden the solution.
entry_criteria: Core Build wave exit criteria met
End-to-end staging execution verified at least once
exit_criteria: Exponential-backoff retry (3 retries, 45s timeout) verified under simulated TRAPI failure
Conditional PUT (If-None-Match) prevents duplicate blob writes on retrigger
All three Application Insights alert rules active and tested
No hardcoded secrets; all config via Key Vault references or Managed Identity
Integration test asserts Mattingly, Winfield, Henderson present in blob output
Security scan (Bandit/Checkov) passes in CI

waves.name: Release Readiness
waves.objective: Validate the full production deployment, smoke-test the nightly schedule, activate monitoring, and complete documentation.
entry_criteria: All unit and integration tests passing in CI
Monitoring alerts configured and validated in staging
exit_criteria: Production deployment completed via staging slot swap in GitHub Actions
First nightly execution (2 AM UTC) completes successfully in production
All three alert rules active in production Application Insights
README documents local dev setup, TRAPI auth, and blob naming convention
Post-deployment smoke test passing

tasks.title: Review and approve implementation task plan
tasks.description: Review this task plan for completeness, sequencing, and alignment with spec.md. Confirm TRAPI auth mechanism, endpoint, player scope, and networking assumptions before approving. Dependency graph is included below.
outputs: Resolved open questions (TRAPI auth, endpoint, player scope, networking, plan tier)

Visual dependency graph:

PROJ-01 (no deps)
INF-01 (no deps)
INF-02 → INF-01
INF-03 → INF-01
INF-04 → INF-01, INF-02, INF-03
INF-05 → INF-04
SEC-01 → INF-01, INF-02, INF-03
SEC-02 → INF-02, INF-03
REL-01 → PROJ-01
APP-01 → PROJ-01, INF-04, SEC-01
APP-02 → APP-01
APP-03 → APP-01, INF-04, SEC-02
APP-04 → APP-02, APP-03
APP-05 → APP-04
OBS-01 → INF-05, APP-04
OBS-02 → INF-05, APP-04
QA-01 → APP-01, APP-02, APP-03
QA-02 → APP-04, QA-01
QA-03 → APP-04
DOC-01 → APP-04, QA-02
REL-02 → REL-01, QA-02, OBS-01, OBS-02, DOC-01
acceptance_criteria: All open questions triaged with owners and resolution dates assigned
Task plan approved by rciapala
TRAPI auth mechanism confirmed (Managed Identity or Key Vault secret)
Player scope confirmed (active roster only vs. broader staff)
risks: TRAPI auth mechanism unknown — blocks APP-01 if not resolved before Core Build wave
Player scope ambiguity affects prompt template and validation thresholds

tasks.title: Provision resource group and base Bicep scaffolding
tasks.description: Extend or create /infra/main.bicep to orchestrate all modules (storage, functionapp, keyvault, monitoring, alerts, rbac). Ensure parameter files exist for staging and production environments. Output resource names and IDs.
acceptance_criteria: main.bicep deploys without errors to a clean resource group
Parameter files exist for staging and production with correct values
All module outputs (resource names, IDs) are exposed as main.bicep outputs
Tags project=1985-NY-Yankees and owner=rciapala applied to all resources
risks: Existing partial Bicep modules may conflict; audit /infra before extending

tasks.title: Provision dedicated storage account and yankees-roster container
tasks.description: Add or update /infra/modules/storage.bicep to provision a dedicated Standard_LRS storage account (Cool access tier, allowSharedKeyAccess:false, soft-delete 7 days) with a private yankees-roster container. Separate from the Function App host storage account.
acceptance_criteria: Dedicated storage account provisioned with Standard_LRS and Cool access tier
allowSharedKeyAccess set to false
Blob soft-delete enabled with 7-day retention
yankees-roster container created with publicAccess:None
Storage account is distinct from the Function App host storage account
risks: allowSharedKeyAccess:false requires all clients to use identity-based auth — confirm SDK support

tasks.title: Provision Azure Key Vault for TRAPI credentials
tasks.description: Add /infra/modules/keyvault.bicep to provision a zone-redundant Key Vault. Wire Key Vault URI into Function App settings as a Key Vault reference. Provision only if TRAPI does not support Managed Identity auth.
inputs: PROJ-01 resolution of TRAPI auth question
acceptance_criteria: Key Vault provisioned with zone-redundant SKU
Soft-delete and purge protection enabled
Key Vault URI exposed as an output from main.bicep
Function App app setting references Key Vault secret via @Microsoft.KeyVault() syntax
risks: If TRAPI uses Managed Identity, Key Vault may be unused but should still be provisioned for future secrets

tasks.title: Provision Function App with system-assigned Managed Identity
tasks.description: Update /infra/modules/functionapp.bicep to provision a Consumption Plan Linux Python 3.11 Function App with system-assigned Managed Identity, APPLICATIONINSIGHTS_CONNECTION_STRING, AzureWebJobsStorage__accountName (identity-based), functionTimeout=120s, and TRAPI endpoint/scope app settings. Include staging slot configuration.
inputs: INF-05 App Insights connection string output
acceptance_criteria: Function App provisions on Consumption Plan with Python 3.11 (Linux)
System-assigned Managed Identity enabled
APPLICATIONINSIGHTS_CONNECTION_STRING wired from monitoring module output
AzureWebJobsStorage__accountName set (no listKeys/shared key)
TRAPI_ENDPOINT and TRAPI_AUTH_SCOPE app settings present (value from Key Vault reference or param)
functionTimeout set to 120 in host.json (managed via app artifact, not Bicep)
Staging slot provisioned with slot-specific settings swapped correctly
risks: Consumption Plan may not support deployment slots — verify before implementing slot swap CI/CD

tasks.title: Provision Application Insights workspace and alert rules
tasks.description: Update /infra/modules/monitoring.bicep for workspace-based Application Insights (30-day retention, sampling enabled) and extend /infra/modules/alerts.bicep with three alert rules: execution failure (>0 in 1h), duration (>90s), and data quality (player_count_returned <24 or >40). Wire email action group to rciapala.
acceptance_criteria: Workspace-based Application Insights provisioned with 30-day retention
Adaptive sampling enabled to control ingestion cost
Execution failure alert fires when failures > 0 in a 1-hour window
Duration alert fires when execution duration > 90 seconds
Data quality alert fires when player_count_returned < 24 or > 40
Email action group notifies rciapala for all three alerts
risks: Custom metric alert (player_count_returned) requires the metric to be emitted before the alert can be fully tested

tasks.title: Assign Storage Blob Data Contributor RBAC to Managed Identity
tasks.description: Update /infra/modules/rbac.bicep to assign Storage Blob Data Contributor to the Function App Managed Identity scoped to the yankees-roster container (not account-level). Also assign Storage Blob Data Contributor on the host storage account for AzureWebJobsStorage identity-based access.
inputs: INF-02 storage account and container resource IDs
acceptance_criteria: Storage Blob Data Contributor assigned to Managed Identity scoped to yankees-roster container
Storage Blob Data Contributor and Queue Data Contributor assigned on host storage for AzureWebJobsStorage
No account-level overly permissive role assignments
RBAC assignments verified via az role assignment list in CI smoke test
risks: Container-scoped RBAC requires the container to exist before assignment — dependency on INF-02

tasks.title: Assign Key Vault Secrets User RBAC to Managed Identity
tasks.description: Extend /infra/modules/rbac.bicep to assign Key Vault Secrets User role to the Function App Managed Identity on the Key Vault provisioned in INF-03. Store TRAPI credentials as Key Vault secrets if Managed Identity auth is unsupported.
acceptance_criteria: Key Vault Secrets User assigned to Managed Identity on the Key Vault
Function App can read TRAPI secret from Key Vault via Key Vault reference in app settings
No hardcoded secrets in source code or app settings
Verified via az keyvault secret show using Managed Identity token in staging
risks: If TRAPI supports Managed Identity natively, Key Vault secret management can be skipped but RBAC assignment should remain for future use

tasks.title: Set up GitHub Actions CI/CD pipeline with staging slot swap
tasks.description: Create .github/workflows/ci.yml (lint, unit tests on PR) and .github/workflows/cd.yml (deploy to staging slot on push to main, run smoke test, swap to production). Use OIDC federated identity for Azure authentication. Include Bicep deployment step before function app deployment.
acceptance_criteria: CI workflow triggers on PR and runs lint (flake8/ruff) and unit tests with pytest
CD workflow triggers on push to main and deploys Bicep infrastructure first
Function app deployed to staging slot before production
Smoke test executed against staging slot before slot swap
Slot swap only proceeds if smoke test passes
OIDC federated credentials used (no long-lived service principal secrets in GitHub)
Pipeline fails fast on any step failure
risks: Consumption Plan may not support deployment slots — fallback to separate staging Function App required
OIDC federation setup requires Azure AD app registration with federated credential

tasks.title: Implement TRAPI client with retry and timeout
tasks.description: Implement or update /app/trapi_client.py to send authenticated HTTP POST requests to the TRAPI GPT-4o endpoint using Managed Identity bearer tokens (DefaultAzureCredential). Enforce 45-second timeout and 3 exponential-backoff retries. Pin to a specific GPT-4o model version via the request payload.
inputs: PROJ-01 TRAPI endpoint and auth scope
acceptance_criteria: TRAPI client authenticates via DefaultAzureCredential with the confirmed TRAPI auth scope
HTTP POST timeout enforced at 45 seconds
Exponential-backoff retry with 3 attempts on transient failures (5xx, timeout)
GPT-4o model version pinned in request payload
Prompt loaded from prompts/get_1985_yankees.txt at runtime
Module-level credential reuse (no new credential per call)
Unit tests mock HTTP layer and assert retry/timeout behavior
risks: TRAPI auth scope unknown until PROJ-01 resolved — cannot test against real endpoint until confirmed
TRAPI rate limits or model version availability may affect reliability

tasks.title: Create pinned prompt template for 1985 Yankees roster
tasks.description: Create prompts/get_1985_yankees.txt with a structured prompt that instructs GPT-4o to return the 1985 New York Yankees active roster in the required JSON schema. Prompt must be version-controlled and the TRAPI client must load it from disk at runtime.
acceptance_criteria: prompts/get_1985_yankees.txt exists in source control
Prompt instructs GPT-4o to return JSON matching the players schema (name, position, jersey_number)
Prompt explicitly requests only the 1985 active roster (or agreed scope from PROJ-01)
TRAPI client loads prompt from file path at runtime (not hardcoded inline)
Prompt hash logged via trapi_request_sent structured log event
risks: GPT-4o may return inconsistent formats across calls if prompt is under-specified — iterate prompt until schema compliance is reliable

tasks.title: Implement blob writer with conditional PUT and Managed Identity auth
tasks.description: Implement or update /app/blob_writer.py to write validated roster JSON to the dedicated storage account using DefaultAzureCredential. Use date-stamped blob name (UTC) and conditional PUT (If-None-Match: *) to prevent duplicate writes. Write failed/invalid responses to failed/ prefix.
acceptance_criteria: Blob written to yankees-roster/{run_date_utc}.json using UTC date
DefaultAzureCredential used — no connection strings or shared keys
Conditional PUT (If-None-Match: *) raises ResourceExistsError on duplicate same-day write
Failed/invalid responses written to yankees-roster/failed/{run_date_utc}.json
blob_write_succeeded log event emitted with blob URI
Unit tests assert conditional PUT behavior and failed/ prefix routing
risks: allowSharedKeyAccess:false on storage requires SDK version that supports identity-based auth for all operations

tasks.title: Implement main function with schema validation and Timer Trigger
tasks.description: Implement /app/function_app.py with the GetAndStoreYankeesRoster Timer Trigger (schedule 0 0 2 * * *, useMonitor:true, UTC). Orchestrate TRAPI call → schema validation (24–28 player count, required fields) → blob write or failure path. Emit all structured log events and player_count_returned custom metric.
acceptance_criteria: Timer Trigger schedule set to 0 0 2 * * * (UTC)
useMonitor:true set in trigger configuration
host.json sets functionTimeout to 120 seconds and maxConcurrentCalls to 1
Schema validation checks player count is 24–28 and each player has name, position, jersey_number fields
Failure path writes to failed/ prefix and logs structured error event
player_count_returned custom metric emitted via Application Insights on each run
All five structured log events emitted: function_started, trapi_request_sent, trapi_response_received, blob_write_succeeded, function_completed
risks: Validation threshold (24–28) may need adjustment if player scope is broadened per PROJ-01

tasks.title: Add OpenTelemetry structured logging integration
tasks.description: Integrate OpenTelemetry SDK into the Function App for structured logging. Ensure all key log events include required fields (model version, prompt hash, token count, latency ms, player count, blob URI). Configure Application Insights exporter via APPLICATIONINSIGHTS_CONNECTION_STRING.
acceptance_criteria: OpenTelemetry SDK configured with Azure Monitor exporter
All five structured log events include required contextual fields
Logs visible in Application Insights Live Metrics and Logs (KQL queryable)
player_count_returned emitted as a custom metric dimension in Application Insights
No sensitive data (tokens, credentials) logged
risks: OpenTelemetry Azure Monitor exporter version compatibility with Python 3.11 and azure-functions SDK

tasks.title: Validate Application Insights alert rules in staging
tasks.description: Test all three Application Insights alert rules (execution failure, duration, data quality) in the staging environment by simulating failure conditions. Confirm email notifications are received by rciapala. Validate player_count_returned custom metric appears in Application Insights.
outputs: Runbook entry confirming alert validation results
acceptance_criteria: Execution failure alert fires within 5 minutes of a simulated function failure in staging
Duration alert fires when execution duration exceeds 90 seconds (simulated via sleep)
Data quality alert fires when player_count_returned custom metric is < 24 (simulated)
Email notification received by rciapala for each alert
player_count_returned metric visible in Application Insights Metrics explorer
risks: Custom metric alert requires metric to be emitted at least once before alert evaluation activates

tasks.title: Create Application Insights KQL queries and dashboard
tasks.description: Create saved KQL queries in Application Insights for key operational scenarios: daily execution success/failure, player_count_returned trend, TRAPI latency histogram, and failed blob writes. Optionally create a workbook for operational visibility.
outputs: KQL queries documented in /infra/README.md or a dedicated ops runbook
acceptance_criteria: KQL query returns daily execution results (success/failure count) for the last 30 days
KQL query returns player_count_returned trend over time
KQL query returns TRAPI latency p50/p95 from trapi_response_received events
KQL query returns all entries in failed/ blob prefix correlated with function invocations
Queries documented and runnable by any team member
risks: Custom structured log fields require consistent schema in APP-05 for KQL to work reliably

tasks.title: Write unit tests for TRAPI client, schema validator, and blob writer
tasks.description: Write pytest unit tests in /tests/ for trapi_client.py (mock HTTP, assert retry/timeout), schema validation logic (valid/invalid player counts, missing fields), and blob_writer.py (conditional PUT, failed/ prefix routing). Target >80% coverage on these modules.
acceptance_criteria: Unit tests cover TRAPI client retry behavior (3 retries, exponential backoff)
Unit tests cover 45-second timeout enforcement
Unit tests cover schema validation: valid roster (24–28), too few (<24), too many (>28), missing fields
Unit tests cover blob writer conditional PUT (success and ResourceExistsError on duplicate)
Unit tests cover failed/ prefix write on validation failure
All tests pass in CI (python -m pytest tests/ -v --cov)
Coverage report shows >80% for trapi_client, blob_writer, and validation modules
risks: Mocking DefaultAzureCredential and BlobServiceClient requires careful fixture design to avoid test brittleness

tasks.title: Write integration test asserting known players in blob output
tasks.description: Write an integration test (runnable in staging) that triggers the function, waits for blob output, and asserts that Don Mattingly, Dave Winfield, and Rickey Henderson are present in the roster JSON. Test should be runnable as a post-deployment smoke test in CI.
acceptance_criteria: Integration test triggers function via Azure Functions admin API or waits for scheduled trigger
Test reads blob from yankees-roster/{today_utc}.json in staging storage account
Assertions: Don Mattingly, Dave Winfield, and Rickey Henderson all present by name
Assertion: player count is between 24 and 28
Assertion: each player object has name, position, and jersey_number fields
Test is executable as a CI step (requires staging environment credentials)
risks: Integration test requires live TRAPI access in staging — blocked until TRAPI auth confirmed (PROJ-01)
Test may be flaky if GPT-4o returns variable player sets — prompt hardening (APP-02) is a prerequisite

tasks.title: Run security scan (Bandit and Checkov) in CI
tasks.description: Add Bandit (Python SAST) and Checkov (Bicep/IaC scan) steps to the CI pipeline. Fail the build on high-severity findings. Verify no hardcoded secrets, no overly permissive RBAC, and no insecure HTTP calls.
acceptance_criteria: Bandit scan passes with no high-severity findings on /app source code
Checkov scan passes on all Bicep templates in /infra with no critical findings
CI pipeline fails if either scan reports high/critical findings
No hardcoded secrets detected in source tree
risks: Checkov may flag allowSharedKeyAccess:false as a false positive or flag other Bicep patterns — review and suppress with justification

tasks.title: Write README with local dev setup, TRAPI auth, and blob naming
tasks.description: Update readme.md to include: local development setup (Python 3.11, azure-functions-core-tools, local.settings.json configuration), TRAPI auth instructions (Managed Identity or Key Vault secret setup), blob naming convention, how to run tests, and how to trigger the function manually.
acceptance_criteria: README includes step-by-step local dev setup using azure-functions-core-tools
README documents TRAPI auth setup (Managed Identity scope or Key Vault secret path)
README documents blob naming convention (yankees-roster/{run_date_utc}.json)
README documents how to run unit tests (pytest command)
README documents how to trigger function manually for testing
README documents environment variables required in local.settings.json
risks: TRAPI auth documentation depends on resolution of PROJ-01 open question

tasks.title: Execute production deployment and verify first nightly run
tasks.description: Execute the CD pipeline to deploy to staging slot, run integration smoke test, swap to production. Monitor Application Insights for the first 2 AM UTC execution. Confirm all three alerts are active. Capture post-deployment sign-off from rciapala.
acceptance_criteria: CD pipeline deploys to staging slot without errors
Integration smoke test passes against staging slot before swap
Slot swap completes and production function app serves the new version
First nightly execution (2 AM UTC) completes successfully and blob appears in yankees-roster container
All three Application Insights alert rules active in production
player_count_returned metric visible in production Application Insights
No critical errors in Application Insights within 24 hours of deployment
Sign-off received from rciapala
risks: Consumption Plan cold-start may cause first execution delay if Premium Plan not used
TRAPI unavailability at 2 AM UTC would trigger failure alert on first run
